close_reason: PR #73 merged. Replaced ~40 domain-mapping 'as T' casts across 5 store repositories with Zod-on-read validation. Two-phase: flat *RowSchema.safeParse for DB drift + per-column JSON.parse with precise errors + domain schema safeParse. 4 new malformed-row tests, 90/90 store tests pass. 17 aggregation-only casts retained (COUNT/GROUP BY scalars with no Zod schema).
description: In packages/store/src/repositories/*, better-sqlite3's .get()/.all() return unknown. The current pattern uses 'as <T>' casts on JSON.parse(row.data) plus field-by-field casting. This is a soft form of type-lying — if a DB row is malformed, the cast silently widens to the domain type and bugs surface far from the source. Options: (a) Zod .parse() on every read (performance impact on hot paths), (b) codegen row-to-domain mappers from schema, (c) selective Zod on untrusted/rare reads only. Investigate cost/benefit, pick one. Finding from PR #57.
title: refactor(store): eliminate ~40 SQLite deserialize type casts via Zod-on-read or generated row mappers

close_reason: PR #68 merged. Extracted appendOptionalFilters() helper in memory-repository.ts; FTS5 + LIKE search paths now share tenant/category filter construction while keeping query structure separate. Net −12 lines. 86 store tests pass. Gemini suggested widening param type to any — declined (regression vs quality-5 sweep); widen to union when a non-string caller actually appears.
description: Two similar filter/map paths at packages/store/src/repositories/memory-repository.ts:183-270. Agent #1 (DRY) marked MEDIUM because the two query paths have subtle differences (likely FTS5 vs LIKE fallback) — unifying safely requires reading the diff carefully. Needs attention because it's in a hot path. Finding from PR #58.
title: refactor(store): investigate 22-line duplication in memory-repository.ts lines 183-270 (filter/map on search results)

acceptance_criteria: pnpm --filter edge-daemon exec tsx src/main.ts <subcommand> works for all four; exit codes correct
close_reason: PR #42 merged (commit 74587aa). New apps/edge-daemon/src/cli.ts dispatches start/stop/status/run-once via process.argv slice — no added deps. Decisions: default subcommand=start, stop exits 0 on stale-lock cleanup, status always exits 0 with structured JSON, run-once skips lock acquisition (one-shot semantics). Tests: 84/84 edge-daemon, 1071/1071 monorepo, validate green.
description: main.ts currently auto-starts with no argv parsing. Add subcommand dispatch: 'start' (current behavior), 'stop' (send SIGTERM to PID in lock file), 'status' (read lock + last-cycle state, print JSON), 'run-once' (single cycle, exit 0). Use minimal argv parsing — no extra deps.
title: edge-daemon: CLI subcommands (start/stop/status/run-once)

acceptance_criteria: When enabled, /healthz returns 200 while running, 503 during stopping; /last-cycle returns latest CycleResult JSON
close_reason: PR #49 merged (d60a68c). Added apps/edge-daemon/src/health-server.ts (HealthServer via node:http, no Fastify). /healthz returns 200 running / 503 stopping; /last-cycle returns latest CycleResult JSON or 404. Port via DAEMON_HEALTH_PORT (default 0=disabled). Fire-and-forget start from EdgeDaemon to preserve sync start() contract. 11 new tests pass.
description: Add optional embedded HTTP server (Node http, no Fastify to keep daemon footprint small). Routes: GET /healthz → {status: 'ok'|'stopping'}, GET /last-cycle → serialized CycleResult. Port via DAEMON_HEALTH_PORT env (default 0 = disabled). Server lifecycle tied to daemon start/stop.
title: edge-daemon: HTTP health endpoint

acceptance_criteria: JSON log lines include timestamp, level, msg, tenantId; pino added as dep; existing daemon tests still pass with logger injection
close_reason: PR #45 merged (6070ab3). New PinoDaemonLogger in apps/edge-daemon/src/pino-logger.ts implements DaemonLogger via pino with JSON output (DAEMON_LOG_PRETTY=1 enables pretty for dev). main.ts now seeds a child logger bound with {tenantId} so all runtime lines carry the field. Existing ConsoleDaemonLogger + NullLogger preserved as alternatives. 5 new tests + 16 pre-existing pass.
description: Replace ConsoleDaemonLogger with a pino-backed DaemonLogger implementation. Keep DaemonLogger interface unchanged (info/warn/error/debug). JSON output by default; DAEMON_LOG_PRETTY=1 for dev pretty-print. Preserve tests by keeping NullLogger + ConsoleDaemonLogger as alternatives.
title: edge-daemon: structured logging via pino

acceptance_criteria: Cycle retries transient qmd/export errors up to config.maxRetries; test with flaky mock adapter; non-transient errors not retried
close_reason: PR #47 merged (7d42799). apps/edge-daemon/src/retry.ts with classifyError + withRetry; cycle.ts steps 3/4 wrapped. DaemonConfig gets maxRetries/retryBaseDelayMs/retryMaxJitterMs/sleepFn. Full-jitter backoff. 16 retry + 3 cycle tests.
description: Currently each step catches its error and continues. Add per-step retry with exponential backoff + jitter for transient categories (qmd unreachable, export git push conflicts). Max retries configurable via DaemonConfig; non-transient errors bypass retry.
title: edge-daemon: transient-failure backoff in cycle steps

close_reason: PR #62 merged. EdgeDaemon tracks _healthServerStartPromise and stop() awaits it before calling _healthServer.stop(), preventing http.Server leak when stop() is called during startup. 5 race tests in daemon-lifecycle.test.ts pass. Gemini approved + addressed 2 green nits (arrow shorthand in .then, async rm in afterEach).
description: EdgeDaemon.start() calls HealthServer.start() without awaiting. If daemon.stop() fires before HealthServer.start() resolves (signal during startup, or fast test teardown), _server is still null inside HealthServer — stop() returns immediately. When start() later resolves, _server is set and the underlying http.Server runs forever with no one to close it. Fix: track the in-flight start Promise on EdgeDaemon and await it inside stop() before calling _healthServer.stop(), or refactor to make the start/stop lifecycle synchronizable.
title: fix(edge-daemon): health server fire-and-forget start leaks http.Server if stop() called during startup

close_reason: PR #67 merged. Systemd unit overrides DAEMON_SPOOL_DIR + DAEMON_EXPORT_DIR + DAEMON_PID_FILE to /var/lib/edge-daemon/* so defaults work with ProtectHome=true. Gemini approved + addressed nit (comment listing). Also dropped accidental worktree-submodule entries.
description: Unit file sets ProtectSystem=strict + ReadWritePaths=/var/lib/edge-daemon /tmp with ProtectHome=true. Default DAEMON_SPOOL_DIR resolves under ~/.qmd-team-intent-kb/spool which is blocked. Operators who don't override DAEMON_SPOOL_DIR via EnvironmentFile will see silent permission errors. Fix: EnvironmentFile template should set DAEMON_SPOOL_DIR=/var/lib/edge-daemon/spool explicitly, or unit should include home path in ReadWritePaths, or runbook must strongly warn about the override requirement.
title: fix(edge-daemon): systemd unit ProtectHome=true blocks default spool path under user home

close_reason: PR #74 merged. rr5: fixture cleanup loops in 3 repo-resolver test files now guard each cleanup and throw AggregateError after loop completes (no more leaked tmpdirs on partial failure). 87p: gemini-review workflow timeout-minutes 7→15, maxSessionTurns 25→18 — fixes the 9-12 min Gemini runs that were getting hard-killed. Validate green; merged on validate gate (Gemini in progress).
description: Since PR #59 restored the Gemini review prompt + MCP server, reviews on real code changes (#60, #61, #62) take 7-10 minutes versus the old ~2-3 minutes. Validate check returns in <1 min while gemini hangs. Options: raise workflow timeout-minutes from 7 (already raised that during restoration), lower maxSessionTurns from 25, or profile where Gemini is spending time via OTEL output. Low priority — the workflow does complete; this is quality-of-life. Finding from this session.
title: investigate(ci): Gemini review runs taking 9+ minutes post-#59 — consider raising timeout or reducing maxSessionTurns

close_reason: PR #79 merged. Added unscoped counter to RepoScopeResult + info-log when >0 candidates bypass scoping. 7/7 repo-scope tests pass. Operators running scopeByRepo=true now have visibility into how weak the isolation actually is.
description: repo-scope.ts passes candidates through unconditionally when repoUrl is absent, even with scopeByRepo=true. The feature flag thus provides weaker isolation than the name suggests. Fix: add unscopedCount to RepoScopeResult and log it at info/debug so operators can see how many candidates effectively bypassed scoping.
title: improve(edge-daemon): repo-scope passthrough for candidates without repoUrl is silent — add counter

Generic T parameter for caller-specified body types (default unknown). Empty-body handling for 204 responses. -65 LOC across two test files. Gemini approved + applied generic suggestion.

title: refactor(api tests): extract HTTP assertion boilerplate shared by candidates.test.ts and policies.test.ts (~120 tokens, 3 clones per jscpd)

close_reason: PR #72 merged. Added knip.json root config (ignoreBinaries tsx+dev, ignoreDependencies pino-pretty in edge-daemon workspace, duplicates rule off for Zod value+type merging). Removed madge devDep (genuinely unused). De-exported FeedbackEntry + MakeContextOptions. Knip exit code now 0. Gemini approved clean.
description: Knip currently reports 4 rejected findings: pino-pretty devDep unused (dynamic transport reference — but #60 moved it to dependencies, rerun to confirm), tsx in all app package.json (pnpm workspace resolution gap), dev in root (script string misidentified as binary), plus 2 duplicate value+type exports (intentional Zod pattern). None are true positives, but all indicate our knip config could be tightened with path/binary allowlists. Finding from PR #56.

description: Dead interface field, probably copy-paste from deterministic-time pattern used elsewhere. Remove to avoid misleading future implementers.
title: improve(edge-daemon): RetryOptions.nowFn is declared but unused in withRetry — remove

close_reason: PR #60 merged. pino-pretty moved from devDependencies to dependencies so pnpm deploy --prod no longer prunes it. DAEMON_LOG_PRETTY=1 now safe in production. Gemini review: 'clean, well-scoped bug fix'.
description: PinoDaemonLogger.buildPino() dynamically requires pino-pretty when env DAEMON_LOG_PRETTY=1 is set. Package is in devDependencies, so pnpm deploy --prod (used by Dockerfile) prunes it. Setting the env in production crashes the process at startup. Fix: move pino-pretty to dependencies OR document it as dev-only in the runbook AND add a guard that refuses DAEMON_LOG_PRETTY=1 when require fails.
title: fix(edge-daemon): pino-pretty in devDependencies crashes production when DAEMON_LOG_PRETTY=1

close_reason: PR #69 merged (d9aecf5). Added DAEMON_HEALTH_HOST env (default 127.0.0.1). health-server.ts accepts host in constructor; daemon.ts passes config.healthHost; Dockerfile sets ENV=0.0.0.0 + EXPOSE 9100; runbook documents 127.0.0.1 vs 0.0.0.0 tradeoff with k8s livenessProbe examples. 16 config tests + 14 health-server tests pass. Gemini approved + applied 🟢 nit (concise parseNonEmptyString).
description: HealthServer.listen binds loopback-only. Inside a container 127.0.0.1 is the container's own loopback — Docker host health probes and k8s liveness/readiness probes cannot reach the endpoint. Since Dockerfile ships with the same epic, this is a real usability gap. Fix: make bind address configurable via DAEMON_HEALTH_HOST env (default 127.0.0.1 for host installs, Dockerfile sets 0.0.0.0).
title: fix(edge-daemon): health server binds only 127.0.0.1 — unreachable from Docker host/k8s probes

close_reason: all steps complete
description: Implement packages/repo-resolver (currently a 1-line stub). Provides deterministic repo context resolution for MemoryCandidate enrichment and tenantId derivation across plain repos, pnpm/npm/yarn workspaces, nx, turborepo, lerna, nested worktrees, and detached HEAD. Consumed by claude-runtime (candidate enrichment) and edge-daemon (optional scoping).
title: Epic: repo-resolver multi-repo context package

close_reason: Filed 000-docs/026-AT-DSGN-repo-resolver-design.md. ADR defines RepoContext and ResolverError types, 6-step resolution procedure (single consolidated git rev-parse call), monorepo detection precedence (pnpm/npm-yarn/nx/turbo/lerna), 4-tier tenantId derivation (env → config → normalized remote URL → basename) with credential stripping, 5-min TTL process-local cache, failure-mode table, security considerations, and fixture-based testing strategy. Index updated with 025/026 entries and next sequence bumped to 027. pnpm format:check clean.
description: Create an ADR in 000-docs/ (use /doc-filing convention, AT-DSGN category) defining: RepoContext type shape, resolution precedence order, tenant derivation strategy, behavior on edge cases (detached HEAD, no-remote, submodules, worktrees, bare repos), caching semantics, and failure modes.
title: repo-resolver: write ADR / design doc

acceptance_criteria: Exports resolveRepoContext; unit tests cover plain repo, detached HEAD, no-remote, non-git cwd; returns typed Result
close_reason: Implemented resolveRepoContext(cwd) in packages/repo-resolver. Returns Result<RepoContext, ResolverError> via common's Result type. Core resolver covers plain repo, no-remote, detached HEAD, bare repo (via rev-parse --absolute-git-dir fallback), no-commits, non-git cwd, and missing path. Single consolidated git rev-parse --show-toplevel --is-bare-repository HEAD probe for the hot path; remote+branch fetched in parallel. realpath canonicalization on cwd. 8/8 package tests pass, 1008/1008 full suite pass under pnpm validate. Monorepo fields default to non-monorepo — to be populated by bead mbd.3.
description: Implement resolveRepoContext(cwd: string): Promise<Result<RepoContext, ResolverError>> returning {repoRoot, repoName, remoteUrl, branch, commitSha, isMonorepo, workspaceRoot, workspacePackage}. Use git shellouts via child_process; handle detached HEAD, no-remote, and non-git cwd gracefully. Use Result<T,E> from common package.
title: repo-resolver: implement resolveRepoContext() core API

acceptance_criteria: Fixture tests for each monorepo tool type pass; correct workspacePackage identified when cwd is inside a workspace subdir
close_reason: Implemented detectMonorepo(cwd, repoRoot) in packages/repo-resolver/src/monorepo.ts. Walks upward from cwd to repoRoot probing for workspace manifests in documented precedence order: pnpm-workspace.yaml → package.json with workspaces field (array or {packages:[]}) → nx.json → turbo.json → lerna.json. workspacePackage derived by walking from cwd back toward workspaceRoot looking for the nearest package.json with a non-empty name field; null when cwd === workspaceRoot or no named package.json exists between. Wired into resolveRepoContext via spread (...monorepo) so RepoContext.isMonorepo/workspaceRoot/workspacePackage now populate. 64KB manifest size cap defends against pathological files. relative-path containment check (isWithin) defends against /repo-foo vs /repo-foo-bar prefix-match bug. 16 monorepo unit tests + 2 resolver-level integration tests using new pnpm-workspace fixture. Full suite 1026/1026 pass under pnpm validate.
description: Walk up from cwd detecting monorepo root and which workspace package contains cwd. Support: pnpm-workspace.yaml, package.json workspaces (npm/yarn), nx.json, turbo.json, lerna.json. Return {isMonorepo, workspaceRoot, workspacePackage}.
title: repo-resolver: monorepo detection

acceptance_criteria: Unit tests cover all 4 precedence tiers; normalization handles ssh/https/git+ssh URL forms
close_reason: Implemented deriveTenantId(ctx, overrides?) and normalizeRemoteUrl() in packages/repo-resolver/src/tenant.ts. 4-tier precedence: explicit override → env var (configurable name, default TEAMKB_TENANT_ID) → .teamkb/config.json tenantId in workspaceRoot ?? repoRoot → normalized remote URL → repo basename. Remote URL normalization strips protocol, user-info credentials, converts scp-style host:path → host/path, preserves explicit ports, strips .git, lowercases. Config reader has 64KB size cap and silently falls through on malformed JSON / missing field / non-string value. Empty/whitespace explicit and env values fall through (don't block downstream). 20 unit tests covering all precedence tiers, all URL forms, credential stripping, malformed config handling, port preservation. Full suite 1028/1028 pass.
description: Implement deriveTenantId(ctx: RepoContext, override?: TenantOverrides): string. Precedence: (1) explicit TEAMKB_TENANT_ID env override, (2) .teamkb/config.json in repoRoot or workspaceRoot, (3) normalized remote URL (strip protocol/auth/.git suffix, lowercase), (4) repoRoot basename fallback. Document precedence in design doc.
title: repo-resolver: tenant ID derivation strategy

acceptance_criteria: Repeated resolveRepoContext() calls on same cwd hit cache; clearCache() resets; TTL expires entries
close_reason: Implemented RepoContextCache class + module-level default singleton in packages/repo-resolver/src/cache.ts. Two-level lookup: realpath(cwd) → repoRoot → {context, fetchedAt}. Repeated resolutions from any subdir of the same repo share a single entry. 5-minute default TTL, configurable via setCacheTtl() or per-instance ttlMs option. Stale entries evicted on access (not background eviction). resolveRepoContext now accepts {cache?: RepoContextCache | null} option: undefined uses default singleton, null disables caching, instance lets advanced callers/tests inject their own. clearCache(), getDefaultCache(), setCacheTtl(), setDefaultCache() exposed for tests and explicit invalidation. Errors not cached (only successful resolutions). 20/20 tests including TTL bounds, cwd index sharing, null cache bypass, error non-caching, expiry refresh, clear() reset. Full suite 1020/1020 pass.
description: Cache resolved RepoContext by resolved repoRoot to avoid repeated git shellouts. Cache is per-process, time-bounded (5 min default, configurable). Expose clearCache() for tests. Cache key: realpath(cwd) → repoRoot mapping, then repoRoot → RepoContext.
title: repo-resolver: process-local cache

acceptance_criteria: claude-runtime tests prove enrichment populates expected fields; fallback to existing behavior when resolver errors
close_reason: PR #43 merged (commit 5640c4a). DefaultContextProvider in packages/claude-runtime/src/capture/context-provider.ts now calls resolveRepoContext() first; on success maps RepoContext → GitContext (repoName, commitSha, tenantId via deriveTenantId); on any error falls back to legacy resolveGitContext() — non-git cwd preserved. Extended GitContext with optional repoName/commitSha. candidate-builder defaults projectContext from gitContext.repoName when capture event lacks explicit value. Tests: 96/96 claude-runtime, 58/58 repo-resolver, full validate green in CI.
description: Wire resolveRepoContext into packages/claude-runtime capture pipeline. MemoryCandidate.projectContext fields (repoName, branch, commitSha) populated from resolver when available. Preserve existing fields if resolver returns error (no regression for non-git cwd).
title: repo-resolver: integrate with claude-runtime candidate enrichment

description: Add tests/ fixtures covering: plain repo, pnpm workspace with nested package, nx workspace, detached HEAD, bare repo, non-git cwd, submodule, git worktree, no remote configured. Use mkdtempSync + git init in beforeEach to build fixtures deterministically.

close_reason: PR #75 merged. EdgeDaemon now has async bootstrap() that resolves repo-context once when scopeByRepo enabled, sync start() preserved (honors 6bk sync-throw contract), cli.ts awaits bootstrap before start. _repoContext stashed and spread into deps per cycle. 145/145 edge-daemon tests pass. Gemini approved+applied depsWithContext simplification nit.
description: Daemon is long-lived and process.cwd() is fixed at startup, so calling resolveRepoContext per-cycle is unnecessary overhead. Resolver has a TTL cache (PR #38) — pass {cache: explicit} or resolve once at daemon start and memoize, rather than relying on the default cache implicitly.
title: improve(edge-daemon): resolveRepoContext called every cycle — make caching explicit

acceptance_criteria: New PR shows Gemini posts a review body (or inline comments); gemini-review check only passes when an actual review was posted
close_reason: PR #59 merged. Restored prompt: '/gemini-review' + mcpServers.github config + env block + concurrency. Subsequent PRs will now receive actual Gemini reviews.
description: PR #34 ('join wild ecosystem shared GCP project', commit 1144c94) reduced .github/workflows/gemini-review.yml from the working template (with prompt + github MCP server config) to a 54-line stub that authenticates to GCP but has no prompt to execute. Action runs clean (check = SUCCESS) but posts zero reviews, zero inline comments. This gave a false-positive 'gemini approved' signal on every PR merged since April 14 — including PRs #44, #45, #46, #47, #48, #49, #50 this session. Fix: re-add prompt: '/gemini-review' and the github MCP server config (per 6e25c27 version) while keeping the wild-ecosystem WIF vars.
title: Restore Gemini review prompt + MCP server stripped by PR #34
